data.body: EVAL-EXECUTION ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED (not a hard blocker): a SINGLE container review runs fine — review_plan on 5fd2 (14 children, default workers=6) COMPLETED in 9min (BLOCK, 2 blocking/7 advisory), exit 0. NOT RAM (128GB), NOT ulimit (unlimited), NOT disk (1.4TB free). The kills were SELF-INFLICTED: my 'ps aux | grep e6_judge...run-b' health checks were FALSE NEGATIVES (real cmd is 'python lowmem_run.py ...'), so I declared live runs dead and spawned DUPLICATE concurrent run-b processes; 2-3 concurrent heavy container reviews is what triggers the SIGKILLs. DISCIPLINE GOING FORWARD: (1) run exactly ONE heavy eval process at a time — strictly sequential E6 -> E2 -> E3; (2) monitor by JOURNAL COUNT (wc -l the raw.jsonl), NEVER by ps process-match; (3) never relaunch until the completion notification fires. lowmem workers=1 wrapper is NOT needed (default works alone) and is slower — dropped. E6 Exp-B resumed cleanly at 10/42, single process, default workers. Evals are WORKABLE sequentially (~2h E6 remaining, then E2 ~several h w/ containers, then E3).
